blocks.content: You now have a mental model for Alloy. The best next step depends on whether you want a Cloud guided path, a workshop-style practice environment, or runnable configuration examples.

## Recommended learning paths

- [Send logs to Grafana Cloud using Alloy](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/send-logs-alloy-loki/) — install Alloy and send system logs to Loki
- [Monitor collector health using Grafana Fleet Management](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/fleet-mgt-monitor-health/) — register a collector and inspect fleet health
- [Categorize a collector fleet in Fleet Management](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/categorize-collector-fleet/) — organize collectors with remote attributes

## Workshops and examples

- [Grafana Alloy for Beginners playlist](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLDGkOdUX1UjoUmd6Z-lKgGaGzmZvYxRWs) — video series that walks from concepts through infrastructure and application pipelines
- [Grafana Alloy for Beginners workshop repo](https://github.com/grafana/Grafana-Alloy-for-Beginners) — Docker-based learning environment used with the playlist
- [alloy-scenarios](https://github.com/grafana/alloy-scenarios) — self-contained runnable examples for logs, metrics, traces, and more

## Docs to keep nearby

- [Introduction to Grafana Alloy](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/introduction/)
- [Get started](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/get-started/)
- [Tutorials](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/tutorials/)
- [Component reference](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/reference/components/)
In the next milestone, you wrap up this introduction.
